 {"id":"bd-22k8","title":"Release: add CHANGELOG.md (generated from closed beads)","description":"# Goal
Add a first-class `CHANGELOG.md` to the repo and define how it is generated/maintained.

# Background
This repo already tracks work granularly via beads. `br changelog` can generate a changelog from closed issues, but we need a stable, user-facing file committed to git.

# Requirements
- `CHANGELOG.md` must be readable without beads context.
- Entries should be grouped by type (feature/task/bug/chore) and/or by release version.
- The file should be regenerated in a deterministic way (same input → same output), or at least documented if manual edits are expected.

# Proposed Workflow
1) Run `br changelog` and capture output.
2) Post-process minimally (if needed): add headings, link issue IDs, remove noise.
3) Commit `CHANGELOG.md` updates as part of the release cut.

# Automation (optional but recommended)
- Add a CI job or a `just`/`make`-style documented command that regenerates changelog output.
- If we publish GitHub Releases, use the same content for release notes.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists at repo root.
- [ ] `br changelog` output mapping is documented (in `docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] A human can update the changelog during a release with one copy/paste step.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:37.775978210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:17.877205324Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:33:59.407685545Z","close_reason":"Added repo-root CHANGELOG.md + documented br changelog workflow (commit b4f703a)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22k8","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"Workstream: Theme System Implementation","description":"# Workstream: Theme System Implementation

## Goal
Implement theme loading and switching for the TUI, matching Pi Agent's theme system.

## Background
The TypeScript Pi Agent supports themes that control:
- Color palette (foreground, background, accent colors)
- Syntax highlighting colors
- Status line appearance
- Thinking block styling

Themes are stored as JSON files in:
- ~/.pi/agent/themes/ (global)
- .pi/themes/ (project)
- Package themes (installed via pi install)

## Scope

### In Scope
1. Theme file format definition (JSON schema)
2. Theme discovery from standard locations
3. Theme application to TUI components
4. Theme switching via /theme command
5. Default themes (dark, light, solarized)

### Out of Scope
1. Theme creation wizard
2. Real-time theme editing
3. Complex inheritance chains

## Theme Format
```json
{
  "name": "ocean-dark",
  "version": "1.0",
  "colors": {
    "foreground": "#d4d4d4",
    "background": "#1e1e1e",
    "accent": "#007acc",
    "success": "#4ec9b0",
    "warning": "#ce9178",
    "error": "#f44747",
    "muted": "#6a6a6a"
  },
  "syntax": {
    "keyword": "#569cd6",
    "string": "#ce9178",
    "number": "#b5cea8",
    "comment": "#6a9955",
    "function": "#dcdcaa"
  },
  "ui": {
    "border": "#3c3c3c",
    "selection": "#264f78",
    "cursor": "#aeafad"
  }
}
```

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Theme Loading
- Parse theme JSON files
- Discover themes from standard locations
- Validate theme format
- Provide default themes

### Phase 2: Theme Application
- Apply colors to lipgloss styles
- Wire syntax colors to glamour
- Update all TUI components

### Phase 3: Theme Switching
- /theme command to list/switch
- Persist theme preference in settings
- Hot-reload on theme file change (optional)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Theme files parsed correctly
- [ ] Default themes work
- [ ] /theme command works
- [ ] TUI renders with theme colors
- [ ] Settings remembers theme preference

## Files to Create
- src/theme.rs (theme loading/application)
- themes/dark.json (default dark theme)
- themes/light.json (default light theme)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (apply theme to TUI)
- src/tui.rs (apply theme to console output)
- src/config.rs (theme preference setting)","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.349318511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161349662Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161225581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22p","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22r2","title":"Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)

## Context
23 third-party directories from GitHub repos + 7 agent collection directories. These represent the independent ecosystem -- extensions not in the official pi-mono repo or npm.

## Third-Party Extensions
aliou-pi-extensions (59 entry points!), ben-vargas-pi-packages, charles-cooper-pi-extensions, cv-pi-ssh-remote, graffioh-pi-screenshots-picker, graffioh-pi-super-curl, jyaunches-pi-canvas, kcosr-pi-extensions, limouren-agent-things, lsj5031-pi-notification-extension, marckrenn-pi-sub, michalvavra-agents, ogulcancelik-pi-sketch, openclaw-openclaw, pasky-pi-amplike, qualisero-pi-agent-scip, raunovillberg-pi-stuffed, rytswd-direnv, rytswd-questionnaire, rytswd-slow-mode, vtemian-pi-config, w-winter-dot314 (40 entry points!), zenobi-us-pi-dcp

## Multi-Extension Collections
Several repos contain MANY extensions:
- aliou-pi-extensions: 59 individual extension entry points
- w-winter-dot314: 40 individual extension entry points
- These need per-entry-point testing, not per-directory

## Agent Collections
- agents-mikeastock: 6 extension dirs
- agents-wshobson: agent-related extensions

## Acceptance Criteria
- All third-party extensions attempted (including sub-extensions within collections)
- 75%+ pass rate (lower bar because these are less curated)
- Multi-extension collections properly enumerated and tested individually","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.099332737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T21:25:23.820747670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T21:15:41.004863244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.112754832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ay7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.119040241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-7hgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.106187768Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":435,"issue_id":"bd-22r2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Third-party conformance at 78.3% (18/23). Remaining 5 failures are all unfixable: 2 missing local files, 2 missing npm packages, 1 bunfig config error.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:15:39Z"},{"id":438,"issue_id":"bd-22r2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Final: 19/19 testable pass (100%), 4 known-unfixable skipped (2 real-FS readFileSync, 2 missing npm packages). All 3 conformance suites green: official 60/60, community 53/53, third-party 19/19.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:25:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-23c3","title":"Publish sqlmodel-sqlite crate (crates.io readiness + workflow)","description":"# Scope
Repo: `../sqlmodel_rust`
Crate: `sqlmodel-sqlite`

# Dependencies
- Depends on `sqlmodel-core`.
- Depends on `asupersync` being publishable as a versioned dep.

# Steps
- `cargo package -p sqlmodel-sqlite`
- `cargo publish -p sqlmodel-sqlite --dry-run`

# Acceptance
- Dry-run publish succeeds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:30:24.203932865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:28.189387834Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:28.189312985Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates","sqlmodel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23c3","depends_on_id":"bd-2cdo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:32:41.968578335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-23c3","depends_on_id":"bd-cccv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:30:24.226729107Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-23c3","depends_on_id":"bd-u7t7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:32:45.956818766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":452,"issue_id":"bd-23c3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed publish-readiness validation for `sqlmodel-sqlite` in `../sqlmodel_rust`.

 {"id":"bd-23do","title":"Security suite: capability denial matrix (strict/prompt/permissive)","description":"# Goal
Regression suite proving capability policy enforcement for every connector.

# Scope
- Matrix across policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) and capabilities (fs/http/exec/session/ui).
- Verify denied calls return deterministic host_result errors and are logged.

# Acceptance
- Test output includes correlation ids + denied capability key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:05.554813022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.912956598Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.912868073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23do","depends_on_id":"bd-193","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:05.561475860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":633,"issue_id":"bd-23do","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee.
